issues: Found and fixed duplicate checkbox id attributes in the generated card sort setup page by using the GOV.UK checkbox macro item id option.
Follow-up replaced browser/system dialogs for subgroup creation and reset with in-page card sort controls.
Follow-up added a global ResearchOps font stylesheet to prevent browser serif fallback on pages and native controls.
Follow-up blocked card sort completion until at least one card is in a grouping.
Follow-up registered the new card sort setup page in the visual walkthrough coverage catalogue.
Reset-confirmation follow-up changed Reset sort to require an in-page confirmation before resetting.
Kept OpenAI, MCP, Airtable and Mural bundles skipped because this task did not change those integration contracts.
Drag-cancellation follow-up bumped the study session asset version so local browsers fetch the updated controller and CSS.
Drag-cancellation follow-up preserves card order when a dragged card is dropped back onto its original tray or group.
Drag-cancellation follow-up refreshed the local research-operations preview files outside the repository.
Drag-animation follow-up added a subtle rotating drag animation with a reduced-motion override.
Drag-animation follow-up bumped the study session asset version and refreshed the local research-operations preview stylesheet.
Drag-preview follow-up replaced the infinite original-card animation with a custom cursor-following drag preview.
Drag-preview follow-up hides the native browser drag image with dataTransfer.setDragImage so the preview under the cursor is the animated card.
Drag-preview follow-up keeps the original list card unrotated while dragging; it only dims in place.
Drag-preview-centering follow-up positions the custom preview by subtracting half its measured width and height from the pointer coordinates.
Drag-preview-centering follow-up replaces the unattached canvas drag image with an off-screen transparent DOM element to suppress native drag artefacts.
Tray-insertion follow-up inserts cards returned from groups into the tray at the pointer drop position instead of appending them to the bottom.
residualRisks: Card sort result autosave depends on selecting a participant; changes before participant selection stay client-side only.
D1 production migration still needs to be applied during deployment.
